title: Python Operation IR Package Resource Commands

execplan_profile.projection_rule: canonical_core is authoritative for intent, scope, next action, proof, continuation, and closeout.

canonical_core.requested_outcome: Continue #933 by migrating the next small group of generated Python package-resource commands from compatibility runtime handlers to operation-contract execution.
canonical_core.hard_constraints: Stay on the Python lane and this branch. Do not broaden into planning/workspace report commands or non-Python target completion. Do not claim full Python generated CLI completion while compatibility handlers remain.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: Finish or adjust the already-started memory list-skills slice if it remains the smallest valid continuation; otherwise stop and rescope before touching more runtime behavior.
canonical_core.escalate_when: Escalate if the package-resource executor starts needing semantic report policy, generalized control flow, or a broader DSL.
canonical_core.next_action: Reconcile the in-progress list-skills IR work with primitive schemas and generator hygiene, then rerun focused proof before any further command migration.
completion_criteria: memory.list-skills.report is either runtime-consumed with schema-backed primitive steps and generated package artifact proof, or the slice is explicitly rolled back/rescoped.
Python operation execution inventory distinguishes list-files, list-skills, and remaining compatibility handler cases.
Static proof still prevents full Python completion claims while compatibility handlers remain.
Black-box Python generated command conformance remains green.
canonical_core.closeout_decision: archive-but-keep-lane-open unless #933 is fully exhausted, which this slice is not expected to do.

goal: Promote one additional simple package-resource generated Python command, memory list-skills, from compatibility runtime handler to operation IR execution.
Keep #933 decomposed into command groups so each group has clear operation contracts, generated artifacts, inventory status, and conformance proof.
non_goals: Do not migrate memory status, doctor, report, route-report, or promotion-report in this slice.
Do not migrate planning or workspace commands in this slice.
Do not introduce loops, expressions, or broad DSL semantics.
Do not claim the Python lane or #892 epic is complete.

intent.outcome: Package-resource generated Python commands consume operation IR directly where practical.
intent.constraints: Python-only; implementation-independent operation artifacts remain authoritative.
intent.latitude: Implementation may use hand-owned primitive executor code for package resource reads, JSON parsing, payload assembly, and output emission.
intent.escalation: Escalate before moving semantic report commands into the same executor.

execution.next_step: Reconcile the in-progress list-skills IR work with primitive schemas and generator hygiene, then rerun focused proof before any further command migration.
execution.proof: Generated package static proof plus Python conformance and contract tooling.

invariants: generated artifacts are derived from source contracts
compatibility handler residue remains explicit in python_operation_execution_inventory.json

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: Complete the Python side of #892 by moving CLI-visible behavior behind implementation-independent artifacts and thin runtime primitive executors.

required_continuation.required follow-on for the larger intend: Remaining memory, planning, and workspace read-only report commands still need classification and either operation IR promotion or explicit compatibility-handler acceptance.
required_continuation.activation trigger: After memory list-skills slice is proven or consciously abandoned.

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: A second package-resource command can prove filesystem.read and json.parse primitive schemas.
iterative_follow_through.intentionally deferred: Semantic report commands and all non-Python target completion.
iterative_follow_through.discovered implications: Primitive schema coverage must expand before representative IR can use new target-executor primitives.
iterative_follow_through.proof achieved now: Not yet; implementation is interrupted mid-slice.
iterative_follow_through.validation still needed: Contract tooling currently failed until filesystem.read/json.parse schemas and generic generator hygiene are completed.
iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: Memory read-only report command classification after package-resource commands are complete.

intent_interpretation.literal request: Continue with #933 on this same branch.
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: Use #933 to keep migrating generated Python commands from compatibility handlers to operation IR in bounded command groups.
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: Memory package-resource commands, starting with list-skills after list-files.
intent_interpretation.review guidance: Verify the slice did not silently broaden into full Python completion.

execution_bounds.allowed paths: memory list-skills operation/contract/generator/executor/generated package surfaces and planning artifacts
execution_bounds.max changed files: moderate because generated outputs and primitive schemas are expected
execution_bounds.required validation commands: contract tooling, generated package proof, Python conformance, structured inventory, generator freshness
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: before generalizing into a cross-package executor framework
execution_bounds.stop before touching: planning/workspace semantic report implementations or non-Python runtime behavior

stop_conditions.stop when: Proof is green for list-skills or the slice is rolled back/rescoped.
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: If list-skills requires more than package resource read + JSON parse + payload assembly.
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: If additional command groups become necessary to make tests pass.
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: If Docker/Python conformance fails for reasons outside list-skills.

context_budget.live working set: #933, this execplan, python decomposition, operation primitives, memory list-skills/list-files contracts, memory operation executor, generated command package checker.
context_budget.externalize before shift: Update this execplan execution_run and proof_report before context switch.
context_budget.pre-work config pull: uv run agentic-workspace start --task "Continue #933 memory package-resource operation IR slice" --format json
context_budget.pre-work memory pull: Only route memory if implementation touches memory package behavior beyond package-resource listing.
context_budget.tiny resumability note: Resume by fixing primitive schema coverage and generic generator string hygiene, then rerun focused proof.
context_budget.context-shift triggers: Before adding another command group or changing compatibility completion state.

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: A bounded #933 slice with clear proof and no completion overclaim.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: Python-only, operation artifact authority, no hidden compatibility handler completion claim.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Whether list-skills remains viable or should be rolled back.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: If semantic command behavior must be represented before package-resource commands are complete.

post_decomposition_delegation.decision rule: Use an existing worker only for read-only review or focused validation; implementation write scope should remain local unless split cleanly.
post_decomposition_delegation.required evidence: Worker must report exact proof/check gaps and avoid editing code unless assigned a disjoint schema/check scope.

adaptive_assurance.reason: This is a correctness/intent-preservation lane after previous overclaiming of Python completion.

common_risks: runtime code becomes semantic package logic

execution_run.handoff source: User requested #933 continuation on #931 branch, then stopped work and flagged missing planning artifacts.

execution_run.scope touched: memory list-skills package-resource command and supporting primitive/generator/check surfaces
execution_run.changed surfaces: See touched_paths; implementation changes are currently uncommitted.
execution_run.validations run: Focused proof partially run; generated Python conformance passed, contract tooling failed on missing filesystem.read/json.parse primitive schema refs and generator hygiene.
execution_run.result for continuation: Recover by committing planning surfaces first or explicitly deciding how to handle the uncommitted implementation WIP.
execution_run.next step: Finish planning recovery; then resume or roll back implementation by explicit decision.

closure_check.accepted values: Do not archive until proof is green or slice is explicitly abandoned.
closure_check.why this decision is honest: The work is mid-implementation and #933 remains broad.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: Working tree WIP and failed proof details are recorded.

memory_learning_capture.future agents should not rediscover: Planning state must be updated before starting major decomposed epic slices.
memory_learning_capture.reason: Current issue is planning recovery, not durable package behavior.

durable_residue.learned constraint: When continuing a decomposed epic, promote the concrete slice into state.toml and an execplan before implementation.
durable_residue.motivation worth preserving: The active WIP otherwise becomes invisible to the package's own intent-preservation surfaces.
durable_residue.promotion trigger: If repeated, add a checker that flags #issue implementation WIP without active planning state.
